1.1.name: Socratic method, guided discovery, and direct work

deductions.reason: Tutor enforces constraints (refuses off-topic questions, refuses to write wording) without giving a learning rationale. For example, in turn 5 the tutor says 'I can't write the exact wording for you' and in turn 10 'I can't phrase the label for you' but never explains why doing the work themselves builds their skills. Refusals are framed as policy, not as pedagogy.

Across all 10 turns the tutor never prompts the student to reflect on why a method worked or what changed in their thinking. After the student produces a solid GDP label draft in turn 6 and makes a well-reasoned proxy decision in turn 10, the tutor moves to checking gaps without asking the student to reflect on their process or what they learned about data documentation.

Tutor repeatedly packs 3 or more numbered questions into single responses when fewer focused prompts would be more appropriate. Turn 3 asks three multi-part questions plus a follow-up request. Turn 4 poses three numbered questions each with sub-parts. Turn 6 lists four bullet-point checks. Turn 8 poses three questions plus a follow-up. Turn 9 poses three questions plus two more. This pattern consistently reduces the student's opportunity to think through one issue at a time.

overview: Tutor consistently refused off-topic requests (movies, dinner, music, motivation) across turns 1, 2, 3, and 8 with immediate redirection to the assignment, maintaining strong assignment anchoring throughout.

Tutor maintained excellent Socratic boundaries: refused to write exact wording (turns 5, 10), never gave submission-ready content, and guided student through data decisions via targeted questions rather than direct answers.
Deducted 2 points under 1.3.A.a: tutor enforced constraints as policy ('I can't write the exact wording for you') without explaining the learning rationale for why self-drafting builds skills.
Deducted 2 points under 1.3.D.a: no reflection prompts appeared across 10 turns, even after clear student breakthroughs like the well-formed GDP label draft in turn 6.
Deducted 4 points under 3.1.A.a: tutor consistently packed 3+ questions per response (turns 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 9), reducing student opportunity to focus on one issue at a time.
